2.16.title: Prompt compression
2.16.change_description: Ablation harness that deletes each system-prompt section and measures score impact. Systematically delete prompt sections to identify essential components and find dead weight.
2.16.metric: Score delta per removed section (finds dead weight)
2.16.hypothesis: Removing 50% of non-essential prompt language won't degrade synthesis quality

2.17.title: System vs user prompt placement
2.17.change_description: Parameterize where schema / data / instructions live across the two message roles. Three layouts. Measure cache-hit rates and quality outcomes.
2.17.hypothesis: Restructuring prompt layers optimizes both cached-prefix efficiency and output quality

2.18.title: Prompt prefix caching
2.18.change_description: Mark static prompt sections with cache_control=ephemeral. Verify cache hits via usage headers. Reorganize prompts to maximize prefix caching by placing static schema/instructions first.
2.18.hypothesis: Cost reductions at scale through cached prompt prefixes on immutable schema portions

1.23.title: Internalized contradictions
1.23.change_description: Add contradictions: list[str] (2-4 items). Prompt elicits them. Twin references them for character consistency under probing. Forces synthesizer to surface internal tensions per persona.
1.23.hypothesis: Personas with embedded contradictions feel measurably more human and handle adversarial challenges better

5.08.title: Adversarial detector model
5.08.change_description: Train or prompt a small classifier to distinguish AI personas from real transcripts. Use accuracy as inverted realism metric.
5.08.hypothesis: Detector discrimination is more sensitive than rubric-based judging for evaluating top-tier models

6.09.title: Color palette view
6.09.change_description: UMAP or t-SNE projection of the persona set. Render as HTML trust artifact. Visualize all personas in 2D embedding-space projection.
6.09.hypothesis: Visual distribution patterns correlate with set quality perception and distinctiveness

6.17.title: Persona naming distinctiveness
6.17.change_description: Cluster persona names across all tenants to find archetype collapse (Marketing Mary, Technical Tom). Audit naming patterns for synthetic-origin bias.
6.17.hypothesis: Generic naming indicates low lexical creativity and synthetic-origin bias in persona generation

4.19.title: Cross-language coherence
4.19.change_description: Send user messages in Spanish / Mandarin while persona remains English. Measure code-switching + trait preservation.
4.19.metric: Trait preservation rate; voice collapse rate
4.19.hypothesis: Traits and voice degrade under translation; personality anchors don't reliably cross linguistic boundaries

4.21.title: Curiosity behavior
4.21.change_description: Instruction variant that encourages curiosity (twin asks back). Measure question-asking rate and correlate with realism.
4.21.metric: Question rate vs realism score correlation
4.21.hypothesis: Realistic conversations show symmetric curiosity; one-way personas feel like chatbots

3.23.title: Predictive grounding
3.23.change_description: Need a longitudinal fixture with train/test time split. Synthesize on train, evaluate predictions on held-out records after time T.
3.23.hypothesis: Grounded personas accurately predict future behavior; LLM-prior personas do not

1.12.title: Temporal layering
1.12.change_description: Wrap PersonaV1 in PersonaTemporal with past/present/future slices. Synthesize 3x per cluster with time offset metadata.
1.12.metric: Twin depth rating; responses to 'how did you feel 5 years ago' questions
1.12.hypothesis: Temporal layering deepens perceived authenticity versus single-slice snapshot personas

1.14.title: Belief vs value separation
1.14.change_description: Split into beliefs[] (factual claims) and values[] (epistemic style / how they decide).
1.14.hypothesis: Splitting belief and values reduces contradictions because twins can update facts without breaking reasoning style

3.13.title: Temporal grounding
3.13.change_description: Add date_range field to SourceEvidence. Prompt requires it. Twin reads it for 'is this still true' handling.
3.13.metric: Staleness detection by twin on dated claims
3.13.hypothesis: Time-stamped evidence improves customer confidence and enables longitudinal persona aging

5.09.title: Eval drift over model versions
5.09.change_description: Save eval results per model version. Re-run suite on new releases. Diff. Track judge calibration drift across releases.
5.09.hypothesis: Judges become measurably harsher or kinder with model generations; requires version pinning

1.16.title: Persona-to-persona references
1.16.change_description: Add relates_to: list[{other_persona_id, relation}]. Second pass fills relationships after all personas exist.
1.16.hypothesis: Relational structure improves population-level distinctiveness over isolated persona definitions

2.20.title: Reverse-engineered persona
2.20.change_description: First call generates a 10-turn customer-interview transcript from cluster data. Second call extracts the persona JSON from transcript.
2.20.hypothesis: Dialogue-first approach forces grounding through realistic conversation patterns

3.19.title: Source weighting by recency
3.19.change_description: Add time-decay weight to cluster record rendering. Parameterize decay constant.
3.19.hypothesis: Recent behavioral data dominates persona traits; older data gets systematically deprioritized

5.19.title: Continuous vs binary metrics
5.19.change_description: Run rubric with 1-5 scale vs pass/fail. Compare reliability across both scale types.
5.19.hypothesis: Binary metrics yield more robust signals than scales despite coarser resolution

3.22.title: Domain-specific grounding rules
3.22.change_description: Rule engine that applies stricter grounding for financial/medical claims. N+1 sources required for sensitive categories.
3.22.hypothesis: Strict evidence enforcement reduces false-positive grounding in sensitive claim categories

4.12.title: Twin-to-twin conversations
4.12.change_description: Pair two TwinChat instances from same tenant, alternate messages. Score distinctiveness and personality preservation.
4.12.hypothesis: Twins should remain distinct and recognizable to each other across substantive dialogue

5.23.title: Composite realism score
5.23.change_description: Test weighting schemes for headline 0-100 number from weighted sub-metrics (validity, groundedness, distinctiveness, judge rubric, Turing).
5.23.metric: Stability across model swaps; customer-signal correlation
5.23.hypothesis: Composite metric stability is achievable and correlates with downstream business outcomes

6.14.title: Persona graph relationships
6.14.change_description: Build a graph where edges represent shared traits. Compute density and clustering coefficient as coherence/distinctiveness proxy.
6.14.hypothesis: Graph topology quantifies population-level coherence and reveals persona clustering patterns
